text: Patient is a 76-year-old female admitted to skilled nursing/rehabilitation for reconditioning after ~1 week hospitalization
transcript_quote: You're seventy-six.

text: Generalized weakness/deconditioning with legs unable to support ambulation following hospitalization
transcript_quote: by the end of it my legs just wouldn't answer for me anymore. I couldn't have walked to my own mailbox.

text: History of osteoporosis
transcript_quote: You have osteoporosis — the thin, brittle bones.

text: History of hyperlipidemia (high cholesterol)
transcript_quote: Your cholesterol runs high.

text: History of prediabetes (borderline blood sugar)
transcript_quote: Your blood sugar has been sitting in that in-between zone they call prediabetes.

text: History of multiple lost teeth, chews carefully on one side and eats soft foods
transcript_quote: you've had a hard time with your teeth — quite a few lost over the years.

text: Low back pain radiating to hips, worse with prolonged standing, described as deep ache in the bone
transcript_quote: It's my low back, mostly, and it spreads out to my hips when I stand too long.

text: Pain interferes with sleep on bad nights
transcript_quote: Some nights it kept me from sleep more than the noise did.

text: Standing worsens pain; can turn in bed slowly; requires support to stand
transcript_quote: Standing, I need something to hold and somewhere to aim for.

text: Started naproxen 220 mg tablet for baseline chronic pain
transcript_quote: The first is naproxen — an anti-inflammatory tablet, two hundred twenty milligrams, for that steady baseline ache.

text: Started meperidine 50 mg tablets PRN for severe pain, used carefully and sparingly (e.g., before therapy or bad nights)
transcript_quote: The second is a stronger one called meperidine, fifty milligram tablets, for the worse stretches

text: Counseled that meperidine can cause drowsiness, fogginess, and unsteadiness/fall risk in elderly; nurses to monitor after each dose and use lowest effective dose
transcript_quote: In folks your age it can cause drowsiness, fogginess, unsteadiness on the feet — and I would much rather prevent a fall than treat one.

text: Patient lives alone with minimal social support; sister died two years ago; one neighbor helps with bins
transcript_quote: Nobody, really. I'm on my own... My sister passed two winters back. There's a neighbor who takes my bins out.

text: Screened for domestic violence/safety regarding former marriage; patient reports no current fear or danger, ex-partner long out of life
transcript_quote: No, doctor. He's long gone out of my life, years and years now... there's nobody to be afraid of anymore.

text: Likely chronic undernutrition/reduced intake due to poor dentition and cooking for one
transcript_quote: Cooking for one, and chewing being what it is — supper got small over the years.

text: Social worker referral to arrange home support and community engagement (senior center/community programs)
transcript_quote: a social worker will come visit with you, and I'd like her working on two things: setting up proper support for when you go home, and finding you some regular human faces

text: Dietitian referral for soft-texture, high-protein, calcium-rich diet balanced for prediabetes and hyperlipidemia
transcript_quote: the dietitian is going to be one of your most important visitors here. Soft, manageable textures, but with real nourishment in them — protein especially... and calcium for those bones.

text: Physical therapy ordered for standing, walking, and leg strengthening
transcript_quote: Physical therapy will work with you on standing and walking and building those legs back.

text: Occupational therapy ordered for ADLs (dressing, safe bathing, kitchen tasks)
transcript_quote: occupational therapy will see you as well — they handle the getting-dressed, bathing-safely, kitchen-counter side of life.

text: History and physical examination performed
transcript_quote: Today I do your full history and physical examination

text: Initial nursing admission assessment performed
transcript_quote: the nurses do their own admission assessment

text: Development of individualized plan of care
transcript_quote: the team writes up an individualized plan of care — yours, not a generic one off the shelf.

text: Nursing care with supplementary surveillance every shift monitoring pain, blood pressure, skin, and medications
transcript_quote: Nursing checks happen every day, every shift: your pain, your blood pressure, your skin, your medicines.

text: Fall precautions counseling: use call button before standing at all times due to osteoporosis fall risk
transcript_quote: call button before you stand. Always. Even at two in the morning, even if you feel spry.

text: Pre-discharge assessment planned/completed to evaluate standing, walking, medication and meal management, and home support needs
transcript_quote: before you go anywhere, we do a proper pre-discharge assessment

text: Anticipated length of stay approximately two weeks depending on strength recovery
transcript_quote: Honestly — a couple of weeks, give or take, depending on how the strength comes back.

text: Counseled patient she may report intolerance to meperidine and treatment will be changed
transcript_quote: You can and you must. Say it once and we change course

text: Discharge from hospital documented (transfer from acute hospital)
transcript_quote: She came over from the hospital about an hour ago

text: History and physical note finalized
